questions.question:
A Deployment named `api-fleet` in namespace `affinity-lab` is unschedulable because its placement rules are wrong.

Fix the workload so that:
- it targets Linux nodes with `nodeSelector kubernetes.io/os=linux`
- it keeps `replicas: 1`
- required pod anti-affinity uses topology key `kubernetes.io/hostname`
- the anti-affinity selector matches label `app=api-fleet`
- the topology spread constraint uses `topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname`
- the topology spread constraint uses `maxSkew: 1`
- the topology spread constraint uses `whenUnsatisfiable: ScheduleAnyway`
- the Deployment becomes Available

Do not remove the placement policy entirely. Repair the Deployment scheduling rules.

verification.description: Deployment api-fleet uses the intended node selector, anti-affinity, and topology spread configuration
Deployment api-fleet becomes Available after the placement rules are repaired
The running Pod is scheduled onto a Linux node after the placement repair
